name: Michael Jackson
description: American singer, songwriter, record producer, and dancer (1958–2009)
abstract: Michael Joseph Jackson (August 29, 1958 – June 25, 2009) was an American singer, songwriter, dancer, and philanthropist. Known as the "King of Pop", he is widely regarded as one of the most culturally significant figures of the 20th century.

works.t: Get The Funk Out Ma Face
Get The Funk Out Ma Face
Get The Funk Out Ma Face
Get The Funk Out Ma Face

concerts.label: Benefit: Heal the Kids Initiative
concerts.venue: Isaac Stern Auditorium / Ronald O. Perelman Stage

about_quotes.speaker: Alicia Olatuja
about_quotes.evidence: They only do a few performances a year and they’re loaded. And I had no idea who they were. Does that mean they’re not successful, because they’re not worldwide household name like Michael Jackson? No. And look what happened to him.
about_quotes.doc_title: Alicia Olatuja interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, February 9, 2018

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: I actually played with Michael Jackson and Janet Jackson. I played with the Jacksons for a week at Radio City Hall so I got a chance to perform on the bandstand with those people, Michael Jackson and Janet Jackson.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: George Russell
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewer mentions Michael Jackson in the context of 1990s cultural events; the interviewee (George Russell) does not respond to or mention Michael Jackson.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Russell — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Quincy Jones
about_quotes.evidence: I remember for Thriller, I went to Seth Riggs, who Michael was being coached by. ... We couldn’t have made that album if we hadn’t gone through that process.
about_quotes.doc_title: Quincy Jones — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Allen Toussaint
about_quotes.evidence: I've seen some little youngsters that hasn't been around long enough to have been through too much, and they sing with so much like Michael Jackson.

about_quotes.speaker: Abbey Lincoln
about_quotes.evidence: I don't know what Michael Jackson's saying most of the time... Well, Michael Jackson who killed his ancestors in his face and in his hair, his skin.
about_quotes.doc_title: Abbey Lincoln — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
